Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Mobility of dislocations in FeNiCrCoCu high entropy alloys

Composition_Info.Note: Equiatomic HEA with lattice constant a0 = 3.552 Å from EAM potential

Process_Text.original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential by Farkas and Caro; Random HEA model built by dividing cell into ~4 nm cuboidal cells, each populated with five elements randomly; Energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient method; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration; Simulation cell pre-strained in Y direction via iterative process to relieve thermal expansion stress
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential; random HEA model construction with ~4 nm cell granularity; energy minimization and Nosé-Hoover equilibration

Process_Info.Equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification

Microstructure_Text.original: FCC single-phase solid solution; dislocation cores split into Shockley partial dislocations with connecting stacking fault; significant waviness observed in dislocation core structure due to variable local chemical and energetic environment; potential energy and intrinsic SFE fluctuate locally on the (111) slip plane; screw and edge dislocations exhibit different correlation lengths (screw ~3.2 nm, edge ~5.4 nm at 300 K)
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC solid solution with wavy dislocation cores; partial dislocation splitting with stacking fault; pronounced core waviness from local energy variations

Composition_Info.Note: Ni-rich HEA with increased Ni to 30 at.% while keeping Fe,Cr,Co,Cu at 17.5 at.% each; lattice constant a0 = 3.548 Å

Process_Text.original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential by Farkas and Caro; Random HEA model built by dividing cell into ~4 nm cuboidal cells, each populated with five elements randomly at specified concentrations; Energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient method; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential; Ni-rich composition (30% Ni); random HEA model construction; energy minimization and Nosé-Hoover equilibration

Process_Info.Equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification

Microstructure_Text.original: FCC single-phase solid solution; dislocation cores show high waviness due to variable local chemical environment; dislocation motion studied with four character angles (screw, 30°, 60°, edge); Ni-rich composition shows higher dislocation velocity compared to equiatomic and Cu-rich HEAs
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC solid solution with wavy dislocation cores; Ni-rich composition shows enhanced dislocation mobility

Composition_Info.Note: Cu-rich HEA with increased Cu to 30 at.% while keeping Fe,Ni,Cr,Co at 17.5 at.% each; lattice constant a0 = 3.560 Å

Process_Text.original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential by Farkas and Caro; Random HEA model built by dividing cell into ~4 nm cuboidal cells, each populated with five elements randomly at specified concentrations; Energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient method; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Farkas-Caro EAM potential; Cu-rich composition (30% Cu); random HEA model construction; energy minimization and Nosé-Hoover equilibration

Process_Info.Equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification

Microstructure_Text.original: FCC single-phase solid solution; dislocation cores show high waviness due to variable local chemical environment; Cu-rich composition shows lower dislocation velocity compared to Ni-rich HEA in phonon drag regime; at high stresses, velocity similar to or slightly lower than homogeneous Cu in radiative damping regime
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC solid solution with wavy dislocation cores; Cu-rich composition shows reduced dislocation mobility compared to Ni-rich HEA

Composition_Info.Note: Pure Ni reference; smallest atom size among HEA elements; lattice constant a0 = 3.521 Å (EAM); intrinsic SFE = 123.5 mJ/m²

Process_Text.original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential for Ni (Mishin et al.); dislocation mobility studied with four character angles; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration; constant force applied parallel to Burgers vector on top boundary
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Mishin EAM potential; pure FCC Ni reference; dislocation mobility at 300, 500, 700 K

Process_Info.Equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification

Microstructure_Text.original: Pure FCC Ni with dislocation cores splitting into Shockley partial dislocations; minimal waviness in dislocation cores compared to HEAs; dislocation motion occurs at much lower critical shear stress (~5 MPa) compared to HEAs (~100 MPa); edge dislocation velocity always higher than screw velocity (consistent with homogeneous FCC metals)
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Pure FCC Ni with planar dislocation cores; much lower critical shear stress than HEAs; edge dislocations more mobile than screw dislocations

Composition_Info.Note: Pure Cu reference; largest atom size among HEA elements; lattice constant a0 = 3.621 Å (EAM); intrinsic SFE = 43.2 mJ/m²; SFE similar to HEAs

Process_Text.original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations using LAMMPS; EAM potential for Cu; dislocation mobility studied with four character angles; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat for temperature equilibration; constant force applied parallel to Burgers vector on top boundary
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations using LAMMPS with Cu EAM potential; pure FCC Cu reference; dislocation mobility at 300 K

Process_Info.Equipment: LAMMPS; OVITO for visualization; DXA for dislocation identification

Microstructure_Text.original: Pure FCC Cu with dislocation cores splitting into Shockley partial dislocations; minimal waviness; critical shear stress ~5 MPa; dislocation velocity higher than HEAs at low stress but lower in radiative damping regime; SFE similar to HEAs explaining some similarities in behavior; edge dislocation velocity always higher than screw velocity
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Pure FCC Cu with planar dislocation cores; similar SFE to HEAs; edge dislocations more mobile than screw dislocations
